tenders.title: YPO - 001066 Alternative Fuelled Vehicles DPS
tenders.summary: YPO are looking for Suppliers to be appointed onto a Dynamic Purchasing System (DPS) for the provision of Alternative Fuelled Vehicles. The DPS is designed to meet the needs of all public sector organ
tenders.detailedDescription: YPO are looking for Suppliers to be appointed onto a Dynamic Purchasing System (DPS) for the provision of Alternative Fuelled Vehicles. The DPS is designed to meet the needs of all public sector organisations which includes YPO's internal requirements and any of YPO's wholly owned associated or subsidiary companies. Please note that deadline for submissions for the initial establishment/first round of the DPS is 13th April 2021 at 14:00. Following the initial establishment, the DPS will remain open for any Suppliers to submit a bid at any point until the closing date of the DPS. The DPS will cover the period from 3rd May 2021 to 2nd May 2025 with an option to extend. The decision to extend the contract period will be taken by the end of March 2025, this extension will be up to 23 February 2029.  All extensions will be dependent on satisfactory completion of all aspects of the contract to date, the current market conditions for this category and YPO's contracting structures. The maximum contract period will therefore be from 3rd May 2021 to 23rd February 2029  (subject to an annual review, incorporating price negotiations and KPI performance).

The end customer will place the order/s with the Provider and the Provider will deliver direct to the end customer. The DPS will cover the period from 10 April 2020 to 31 March 2022 with two options to extend for an additional 24 months. After publication the DPS has been amended to extend the opportunity to 24/02/2029. The maximum contract period will therefore be 8.8 years, from 10 April 2020 to 24th February 2029 (subject to an annual review, incorporating price negotiations and KPI performance).

tenders.title: Modeshift STARS
tenders.summary: Delivery of the Modeshift STARS programmes.  Refer UK2: Preliminary market engagement notice - Procurement Act 2023 Notice identifier: 2026/S 000-012356 Procurement identifier (OCID): ocds-h6vhtk-064d
tenders.detailedDescription: Delivery of the Modeshift STARS programmes.  Refer UK2: Preliminary market engagement notice - Procurement Act 2023 Notice identifier: 2026/S 000-012356 Procurement identifier (OCID): ocds-h6vhtk-064dc0  Sheffield City Council use the Modeshift STARS scheme to engage and work with schools on promoting, encouraging, and enabling active journeys to school.   The project aims to: • Engage more Sheffield schools in the project • Increase the number of accredited schools in Sheffield • Support schools to gain higher levels of accreditation • Increase the number of families choosing active journeys to school • Decrease the number of children being driven to school • Deliver active travel initiatives in participating schools across the city,  • Help schools to deliver their own active travel initiatives • Improve air quality around schools by reducing the number of vehicles used on the school run, and parking near the school gates • Make school entrances safer by working with other SCC projects and services (Parking Services, School Streets, Road Safety) • Coordinating and working with associated projects with mutual aims such as Living Streets, Move More, and Local Sports Partnerships.  We currently have 60 accredited schools, with 8 of those being at the highest level.

tenders.detailedDescription: To provide 2nd line support (2LS) for the APHIDS (Access Pass Holder Information Distribution System) solution, maintaining a sustained service. The solution assists in maintaining the security of the UK by delivering a step-change in the ability of the Security Service, the NCA and Police to have an accurate, up-to-date picture of who has access to security restricted areas at all major airports (approximately 50 nationwide) and International Rail hubs, with ongoing efforts to expand to include Air Cargo, and Maritime Ports.

tenders.title: Downs to the Sea and Improving Pulborough and Amberley Projects at RSPB Pulborough Brooks
tenders.organization: ROYAL SOCIETY FOR THE PROTECTION OF BIRDS
tenders.summary: RSPB Pulborough Brooks is a large nature reserve found in West Sussex. It sits within the South Downs National Park and is part of the Arun Valley. Recent works on the reserve have meant we have good
tenders.detailedDescription: RSPB Pulborough Brooks is a large nature reserve found in West Sussex. It sits within the South Downs National Park and is part of the Arun Valley. Recent works on the reserve have meant we have good control of water levels, but there are still improvements to be made. New rills/foot drains will provide better feeding opportunities. Culverting round the willow bed will create a greater area to store water to help sustain the wetland into drier summer months. Bunding will ensure access around the site for management and grazing and will ensure hydrological compartments are clearly defined. This work will help make the site more resilient to the wetter winters and drier summers we are seeing as a result of climate change. Wet features and scrapes will help hold water during drier times and replacing broken culverts will create better water flow and control.

tenders.detailedDescription: The London Borough of Merton (the Council) is inviting tenders to provide quotes for mechanical sweeper brushes. Merton Council operates a mixed fleet of mechanical sweepers for the maintenance of highways, residential roads and town centres. Brushes are required for regular replacement to maintain service efficiency and quality. The brushes will need to be fully compatible with our current mechanical sweeper vehicles.

tenders.detailedDescription: Milton Keynes City Council will soon begin upgrading the bus stops at Milton Keynes Central Rail Station to improve accessibility and safety. These improvements aim to create a safer, more accessible, and user-friendly environment for all passengers.
